title: cosign verify in CI — the gate that lets signed/attested language ship
description: Add a cosign verify CI check; only when green may any signed/attested language ship anywhere.
acceptance_criteria: cosign verify is a required CI check and is green.
notes: Fowler: no attested claim anywhere until this is green (P7).

title: Python signing path for egress / world-changing actions only: DSSE via cosign/sigstore, async/post-response
description: Sign only egress / world-changing actions (PR/issue/deploy/published answer) with a DSSE envelope via cosign/sigstore-python, async and post-response, never on the Slack hot path.
acceptance_criteria: Egress actions produce a DSSE envelope async; the hot path is unaffected.
notes: Torvalds: sign things that change the world, NOT internal monologue; no sign-everything gate (P6).

title: Add intent-eval-core and build the single anti-corruption governance adapter (one import boundary)
description: Add intent-eval-core ~=0.8 and build core/governance.py as the single import boundary consuming its Pydantic models directly.
acceptance_criteria: intent_eval_core is imported only in core/governance.py; version pinned; no GovernanceService wrapper.
notes: Hickey/Fowler: consume Pydantic models directly; NO wrapper; pin the version.

title: Wire the eval harness into CI as a required gate + drift detector
description: Wire the eval harness as a required CI gate plus a drift detector on every behavior-changing PR.
acceptance_criteria: Eval gate is required-green in CI; drift is detected.

title: Golden eval cases per capability asserting output quality, not JSON shape
description: Author golden eval cases per capability (knowledge Q&A, tool selection, issue draft, compliance check) asserting output quality.
acceptance_criteria: A golden case set exists per capability with quality assertions.
notes: Karpathy/Huyen: provenance without evals is a tamper-evident record of being confidently wrong. Build this before trusting any swap/collapse/sign.

title: Pydantic AI agent loop (Bob persona): model-driven tool use, no rigid department
description: Build the thin model-driven agent loop with the Bob persona; one agent + tools, no foreman/8-specialist department.
acceptance_criteria: Bob runs a model-driven tool-use loop end-to-end against any provider.
notes: Karpathy: one agent + tools; process isolation only at real trust boundaries.

title: Redaction scrubber on every sink + planted-fake-key CI test
description: Scrub secrets from every sink (logs, predicates, MCP/A2A envelopes, traces); add a planted-fake-key CI test that fails the build if a key reaches any sink. Artifacts carry key_ref only.
acceptance_criteria: Planted-fake-key CI test is red on leak; emitted predicate shows key_ref never the secret.
notes: CISO: non-negotiable gate before any external demo.

title: LiteLLM provider factory + config/providers.yaml with runtime model selection
description: Build the BYOK model factory (Claude, OpenAI, DeepSeek, GLM/Zhipu, local Ollama/vLLM) selecting provider+model at runtime from env/config.
acceptance_criteria: make_model() returns a working Pydantic AI model for any configured provider chosen via BOB_MODEL/env.
notes: BYOK is a gateway-not-harness concern; no provider hardcoded (P1/P2).

title: Scaffold repo structure, pyproject.toml, CI skeleton, CLAUDE.md, license
description: Stand up the directory tree (core, tools, knowledge, surfaces, eval, config, 000-docs), pyproject with pydantic-ai/litellm/mcp/slack-bolt/intent-eval-core, CI skeleton, CLAUDE.md, license.
acceptance_criteria: Repo builds/imports; make help works; CI skeleton present; pushed.

title: Decide and record the repo name and harness choice as an AT-DECR (Pydantic AI + LiteLLM; greenfield; BYOK)
description: Author the founding decision record: greenfield repo, Pydantic AI + LiteLLM harness, BYOK any provider, consume the IEP kernel.
acceptance_criteria: AT-DECR doc committed under 000-docs with D1-D7 and preserved dissent.
notes: Capture preserved dissent: Goose/Agno considered; canon thinkers defer-caution overridden by owner per decision D6.

title: Make attested true: the signing edge (DSSE + cosign + Rekor) plus the one ecosystem wire
description: Sign egress / world-changing actions only (PR/issue/deploy/published answer) via DSSE + cosign, async off the hot path; cosign verify in CI; Rekor submission; emit signed predicates to the shared audit substrate.
design: Python signing path (sigstore); cosign verify CI gate; Rekor staging then prod; emit into CCS/AGP substrate.
acceptance_criteria: cosign verify round-trips in CI; Rekor entry exists; signing is async; only then does attested language ship.

title: Govern every action: consume the IEP attestation kernel (typed, not yet signed)
description: Consume intent-eval-core through a single anti-corruption boundary; emit typed GateResultV1 predicates; causal delegation linkage; OTel GenAI spans; real token cost. No signing yet.
design: core/governance.py adapter (one import boundary); port policy-gate logic; content-hash delegation chain; OTel semconv; usage_metadata cost.
acceptance_criteria: Predicates validate against intent_eval_core models; OTel spans + real token cost present; NO signed language yet.

title: Prove it works: model-agnostic golden eval set plus CI gate
description: Golden eval cases asserting OUTPUT QUALITY (not JSON shape) per capability, wired as a required CI gate, plus a cross-provider parity eval that is the BYOK correctness proof.
design: Golden cases per capability; eval harness as required CI gate + drift detector; cross-provider parity eval (Claude vs DeepSeek vs GLM vs OpenAI).
acceptance_criteria: Eval set required-green on every behavior-changing PR; parity eval green across providers.

title: Make any key work: BYOK multi-provider gateway, zero-Google-default
description: Any provider via a LiteLLM gateway (Claude, OpenAI, DeepSeek, GLM/Zhipu, local Ollama/vLLM). Zero Google by default. Secrets via env-first then SOPS+age to /dev/shm.
design: LiteLLM factory + providers.yaml; SOPS+age secret resolution; redaction scrubber + planted-key CI test; make run-local under 5 min with zero GCP.
acceptance_criteria: Clean machine with one non-Google key runs Bob in under 5 min; planted-fake-key CI test fails on leak; artifacts carry key_ref not the secret.

title: Build the model-driven agent core: thin loop, MCP-native, Slack surface
description: A thin Pydantic AI agent (Bob persona), model-driven tool use, MCP toolsets for the ecosystem, a provider-neutral knowledge index, and a Slack ingress for dogfooding.
design: Pydantic AI loop; MCP registry (Plane, Twenty, Umami, Gmail, Drive, semantic-scholar); local knowledge index (NOT Vertex); Slack Bolt ingress.
acceptance_criteria: Bob answers in Slack using model-driven tool use; MCP tools reachable; knowledge_search returns from the local index.

title: Stand up the greenfield bobs-brain-v2 repo and record the founding decisions
description: Create the greenfield repo (Pydantic AI + LiteLLM, BYOK, zero-Google-default) and capture the founding decisions. v1 iam-bobs-brain is frozen as ADK-era evidence.
design: Scaffold repo + AT-DECR decision record + lean operating rules replacing v1 R1-R8 + freeze v1.
acceptance_criteria: Repo scaffolded and pushed; AT-DECR and operating-rules docs landed; v1 carries a freeze banner.

title: Final seal of bobs-brain v1: archive notice + pointer to bobs-brain-v2; CI frozen not deleted
description: Seal v1 with an archive notice and pointer to v2; ensure v1 CI is frozen, not deleted.
acceptance_criteria: v1 carries an archive notice + pointer; CI frozen not deleted.

title: Migrate each capability as a tool on Bob, gated by the eval set (collapsed must beat the department)
description: Migrate each capability as a Bob tool, gated by the eval set; collapsed must meet-or-beat the department on quality/cost/latency or it does not ship.
acceptance_criteria: Each migrated capability passes the eval gate and meets-or-beats its v1 ancestor.
notes: Karpathy: collapsed >= department on quality/cost/latency or it does not ship.

title: Capability parity map: each v1 specialist to a tool or sandboxed tool in the new repo
description: Map each v1 specialist (iam-adk/issue/fix-plan/fix-impl/qa/doc/cleanup/index) to a tool or sandboxed tool on Bob.
acceptance_criteria: A parity map exists mapping every v1 specialist to a v2 tool.
notes: Karpathy: 7/8 are one tool in an agent costume; only iam-fix-impl (writes code) keeps process/sandbox isolation.

title: Two-way memory: re-index conversations with value-semantics (immutable facts, mandatory scope, declared conflict policy)
description: Re-index Bob conversations back into the knowledge index as immutable facts (subject/content_hash/asserted_at/asserted_by/supersedes; scope mandatory; conflict-policy declared).
acceptance_criteria: Conversations re-index as immutable facts with mandatory scope and a declared conflict policy.
notes: Hickey/Kleppmann: signing does not fix recall corruption; declare the conflict policy; build it right.

title: AGP/CCS multi-runtime integration: Bob emits predicates AGP consumes; align to CCS signed-journal ordering
description: Integrate with AGP/CCS so Bob predicates are consumed by AGP and aligned to CCS signed-journal ordering.
acceptance_criteria: AGP consumes Bob predicates; ordering aligns to CCS; no competing audit truths.
notes: Kleppmann: Rekor for transparency + content-hash chain for ordering; do not run two competing audit truths.

title: Cross-repo knowledge aggregation: ingest other repos into the knowledge index
description: Ingest other repos into Bob knowledge index for real second-brain recall across the ecosystem.
acceptance_criteria: Bob recalls facts sourced from multiple ingested repos.

title: Plane tools for Bob (search/read/file issues; risk-tiered auto-file on governance violations)
description: Give Bob Plane tools to search/read/file issues, with risk-tiered auto-filing on governance violations.
acceptance_criteria: Bob searches/reads Plane and files risk-tiered issues on violations.

title: Emit signed predicates into the shared audit substrate (CCS/AGP) — the single ecosystem wire
description: Emit signed predicates into the shared CCS/AGP audit substrate so governed second brain wired in is literally true.
acceptance_criteria: Signed predicates land in the CCS/AGP substrate.

title: Rekor submission (staging then production); record rekor_log_indices on the EvidenceBundle
description: Submit DSSE envelopes to Rekor (staging then production) and record rekor_log_indices on the EvidenceBundle.
acceptance_criteria: A Rekor entry exists for signed egress; indices recorded on the bundle.

title: Real token-cost accounting from usage_metadata (no cost=0.01 placeholder)
description: Compute real token cost from usage_metadata to feed budget enforcement and BYOK unit economics. No placeholder cost.
acceptance_criteria: Cost is computed from usage_metadata; no hardcoded placeholder remains.
notes: Huyen: fix the cost placeholder; feeds budget + BYOK unit economics.

title: OTel GenAI semconv spans for every model invoke / tool / MCP call
description: Emit OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic-convention spans for each model invoke, tool call, and MCP call; align, do not invent.
acceptance_criteria: Spans appear per model/tool/MCP call following GenAI semconv.
notes: Gregg/Huyen: extend OTel to eval scores + token cost, not just latency.

title: Causal delegation linkage: every predicate carries delegation_parent = content_hash; one chain per run
description: Add a content-hash causal delegation chain so every predicate carries delegation_parent; one chain per run.
acceptance_criteria: Predicates carry delegation_parent forming one verifiable chain per run.
notes: Kleppmann: Rekor gives non-repudiation NOT ordering; do not treat rekor_log_index as decision order (P10).

title: Port policy-gate logic (risk tiers, mandate, approval) and emit typed GateResultV1 predicate bodies
description: Port the v1 policy-gate logic (risk tiers R0-R4, mandate, approval) and emit typed GateResultV1 predicate bodies. Keep deterministic gate-result/v1 separate from probabilistic judge-decision.
acceptance_criteria: Gate decisions emit valid GateResultV1; deterministic and probabilistic predicates stay separate.
notes: Hickey: keep deterministic gate-result/v1 vs probabilistic judge-decision separate (P9).

title: Cross-provider parity eval (Claude vs DeepSeek vs GLM vs OpenAI) — the BYOK correctness proof
description: Run the same golden cases across providers to prove BYOK correctness.
acceptance_criteria: Parity eval runs the same cases across providers and is green.

title: Slack ingress (Bolt) for dogfooding; Bob answers in-channel
description: Build the Slack Bolt ingress that routes messages to Bob and replies in-channel.
acceptance_criteria: Bob answers messages in a Slack channel.

title: MCP tool registry: wire Plane, Twenty, Umami, Gmail, Drive, semantic-scholar + allowlist
description: Wire the ecosystem MCP toolsets and enforce the allowlist in config/mcp.yaml.
acceptance_criteria: Allowlisted MCP servers load as toolsets; non-allowlisted are refused.
notes: This is how the ecosystem plugs in: tools via MCP, not bespoke clients (P3).

title: Provider-neutral second-brain knowledge index (local/pgvector/LlamaIndex, NOT Vertex) + knowledge_search tool
description: Choose and build a provider-neutral knowledge index and a knowledge_search builtin tool. NOT Vertex.
acceptance_criteria: knowledge_search returns relevant results from the local index; backend documented.

title: make run-local: clone, export any provider key, run Bob with zero GCP in under 5 minutes
description: Wire make run-local so a clean clone with one non-Google key runs Bob end-to-end with zero GCP in under 5 minutes.
acceptance_criteria: On a clean machine: git clone, export KEY, make run-local -> Bob answers, no GCP, under 5 min.

title: Secret resolution: process-env first, else SOPS+age to /dev/shm only; adopt .sops.yaml
description: Resolve secrets from process env first, else decrypt SOPS+age to /dev/shm only, never to disk. Adopt .sops.yaml with the project recipient.
acceptance_criteria: Secrets resolve env-first then SOPS to tmpfs; no plaintext .env committed; round-trip verified.

title: Per-provider smoke tests (each provider answers a fixed prompt with its own key)
description: Smoke test that each configured provider answers a fixed prompt using its own key.
acceptance_criteria: A smoke test exists per provider; skips cleanly when a key is absent.

title: Author the new repo lean operating rules (replace R1-R8): provider-neutral, BYOK-default, MCP-native, governance-via-kernel
description: Write the lean operating-rules standard that replaces v1 Hard Mode R1-R8 with provider-neutral, BYOK, MCP-native, kernel-governed rules.
acceptance_criteria: 002-DR-STND operating-rules doc committed; CLAUDE.md references it.

title: Freeze bobs-brain v1: add README banner and pointer, stop active work, keep as evidence
description: Add a banner to iam-bobs-brain README marking it the v1 ADK-era reference superseded by bobs-brain-v2; stop active work; keep CI frozen not deleted.
acceptance_criteria: v1 README shows the freeze banner with a pointer to v2; no further feature work on v1.
notes: Ward/Torvalds: do NOT port the 185-doc / R1-R8 process apparatus into the new repo.

title: Migrate capabilities off bobs-brain (collapse to tools), then seal v1
description: Map each v1 specialist to a tool (or sandboxed tool), migrate gated by the eval set, then seal v1 with an archive notice and pointer. Collapsed must beat the department on quality/cost/latency or it does not ship.
design: Capability parity map; migrate each as an eval-gated tool; final seal of v1 (CI frozen, not deleted).
acceptance_criteria: Every migrated capability meets-or-beats its v1 ancestor on the eval set; v1 sealed with a pointer to v2.

title: Wire the ecosystem: Plane, cross-repo knowledge, AGP/CCS, two-way memory
description: Plane tools for Bob; cross-repo knowledge aggregation; AGP/CCS multi-runtime integration; two-way memory re-indexed with value-semantics. Sequenced AFTER E6 so each wire rides the signed-predicate substrate.
design: Plane MCP tools with risk-tiered auto-file; ingest other repos into the index; AGP consumes Bob predicates aligned to CCS ordering; immutable-fact memory with declared conflict policy.
acceptance_criteria: Bob files risk-tiered Plane issues; cross-repo recall works; AGP consumes signed predicates; two-way memory stores immutable facts with a declared conflict policy.
